title: Week 10 - Sorting Algorithms I
description: Practice selection sort, bubble sort, and swap counting.

questions.title: Line Up Students by Height from Short to Tall

description.background: In PE class, the teacher needs to reorder a line of students by height from short to tall. Each student's height is stored in the array values in current line order, with n students in total. To get the new line order, sort these heights in ascending order.
description.task: Use the idea of selection sort to arrange the first n heights in values from small to large, then return the sorted array.
description.input: Two parameters: the height array values and the number of students n.
description.hint: In each round, find the smallest value among the unsorted data and swap it to the front of the current round.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if values[j] < values[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        local temp = values[i]
        values[i] = values[minIndex]
        values[minIndex] = temp
    end
    return values
end

questions.title: Organize Game Scores from Low to High

description.background: After a game activity, the system stores player scores in submission order in the array values. To view the score distribution more easily, the administrator wants the scores sorted from low to high, so the lowest score appears first and the highest appears last.
description.task: Use selection sort to organize the first n scores in values from small to large, then return the sorted array.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array values and the count n.
description.hint: Selection sort still works here. In each round, record the position of the smallest value in the remaining data, then swap it with the current position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if values[j] < values[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        local temp = values[i]
        values[i] = values[minIndex]
        values[minIndex] = temp
    end
    return values
end

questions.title: Organize Running Results from Fast to Slow

description.background: After a running test, each student's time is recorded in order in the array values. A smaller running time means a better and faster result. Sort these times from small to large, which means arranging the results from fast to slow.
description.task: Use selection sort to arrange the first n running times in values from small to large, then return the sorted array.
description.input: Two parameters: the time array values and the number of students n.
description.hint: Fast to slow corresponds to time from small to large. In each round, choose the smallest remaining time and put it at the front.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if values[j] < values[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        local temp = values[i]
        values[i] = values[minIndex]
        values[minIndex] = temp
    end
    return values
end

questions.title: Use Selection Sort to Organize a Game Score Ranking

description.background: A game leaderboard needs to display player scores from high to low, with high-scoring players first and low-scoring players later. Player scores are stored in the array scores, with n scores in total. Use selection sort to organize this leaderboard.
description.task: Use selection sort to arrange the first n scores in scores from high to low, then return the sorted scores.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the count n.
description.hint: For descending selection sort, each round should select the largest remaining value and swap it into the current round position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local maxIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if scores[j] > scores[maxIndex] then
                maxIndex = j
            end
        end
        local temp = scores[i]
        scores[i] = scores[maxIndex]
        scores[maxIndex] = temp
    end
    return scores
end

questions.title: Use Selection Sort to Sort Book Numbers from Small to Large

description.background: When organizing bookshelves, the library needs to arrange book numbers from small to large. The current book numbers are stored in the array values, with n numbers in total. Use selection sort to finish the organization.
description.task: Use selection sort to arrange the first n book numbers in values in ascending order, then return the sorted array.
description.input: Two parameters: the number array values and the count n.
description.hint: In each round, first record the position minIndex of the smallest number in the current remaining part, then swap it with the current position.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if values[j] < values[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        local temp = values[i]
        values[i] = values[minIndex]
        values[minIndex] = temp
    end
    return values
end

questions.title: Use Selection Sort to Find the Minimum in Each Remaining Round

description.background: The core process of selection sort is to find the smallest value in the current unsorted data in each round and put it at the front. To observe which number is selected in each round, record the minimum value found in every round.
description.task: Simulate selection sort, save each round's selected minimum value into the array selected, and finally return selected.
description.input: Two parameters: the number array values and the count n.
description.output: Return an array containing the minimum value selected in each round.
description.hint: This result is the same as the ascending sorted array. In each round, after finding minIndex, record values[minIndex] first, then complete the swap.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    local selected = {}
    for i = 1, n do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if values[j] < values[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        selected[i] = values[minIndex]
        local temp = values[i]
        values[i] = values[minIndex]
        values[minIndex] = temp
    end
    return selected
end

questions.title: Count Swaps When Bubble Sorting a Seat Table

description.background: The teacher wants to organize seat numbers from small to large. The current seat numbers are stored in the array seats. When using bubble sort, adjacent numbers are compared repeatedly; if the previous number is larger than the next one, they are swapped. Count how many swaps happen during the full sorting process.
description.task: Use ascending bubble sort to organize the first n seat numbers in seats, then return the number of swaps made during sorting.
description.input: Two parameters: the seat number array seats and the count n.
description.hint: Whenever seats[i] > seats[i + 1], swap these two adjacent elements and add 1 to swaps.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(seats, n)
    local swaps = 0
    for pass = 1, n - 1 do
        for i = 1, n - pass do
            if seats[i] > seats[i + 1] then
                local temp = seats[i]
                seats[i] = seats[i + 1]
                seats[i + 1] = temp
                swaps = swaps + 1
            end
        end
    end
    return swaps
end

questions.title: Use Bubble Sort to Sort Scores from High to Low

description.background: A score ranking needs to display scores from high to low, with higher-scoring students earlier. The scores are stored in the array scores, with n scores in total. Use bubble sort to gradually swap higher scores forward.
description.task: Use bubble sort to arrange the first n scores in scores from high to low, then return the sorted scores.
description.input: Two parameters: the score array scores and the count n.
description.hint: In descending bubble sort, if the previous score is smaller than the next score, swap them.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(scores, n)
    for pass = 1, n - 1 do
        for i = 1, n - pass do
            if scores[i] < scores[i + 1] then
                local temp = scores[i]
                scores[i] = scores[i + 1]
                scores[i + 1] = temp
            end
        end
    end
    return scores
end

questions.title: Compare Swap Counts of Selection Sort and Bubble Sort

description.background: The same number array values may require different numbers of swaps when sorted in ascending order by different sorting methods. To compare selection sort and bubble sort fairly, first copy the original array into two arrays, then sort each copy with one method and count swaps separately.
description.task: Count how many swaps selection sort and bubble sort each make when sorting the first n values in ascending order, then return both results.
description.input: Two parameters: the number array values and the count n.
description.output: Return two values: the selection-sort swap count and the bubble-sort swap count.
description.hint: Copy values into two arrays a and b so one sort does not affect the other. Selection sort counts only when the minimum position changes; bubble sort counts every adjacent-element swap.

questions.reference_code:
function solution(values, n)
    local a = {}
    local b = {}
    for i = 1, n do
        a[i] = values[i]
        b[i] = values[i]
    end

    local selectionSwaps = 0
    for i = 1, n - 1 do
        local minIndex = i
        for j = i + 1, n do
            if a[j] < a[minIndex] then
                minIndex = j
            end
        end
        if minIndex ~= i then
            local temp = a[i]
            a[i] = a[minIndex]
            a[minIndex] = temp
            selectionSwaps = selectionSwaps + 1
        end
    end

    local bubbleSwaps = 0
    for pass = 1, n - 1 do
        for i = 1, n - pass do
            if b[i] > b[i + 1] then
                local temp = b[i]
                b[i] = b[i + 1]
                b[i + 1] = temp
                bubbleSwaps = bubbleSwaps + 1
            end
        end
    end

    return selectionSwaps, bubbleSwaps
end
